# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_device_httpd (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `allow` (String) If you have enabled HTTPD access, specifies the IP address or address range for other systems that can communicate with this system. To specify all addresses, use the value `all`. An IP address can be specified, such as 172.27.1.10. IP ranges can be specified, such as 172.27.*.* or 172.27.0.0/255.255.0.0.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auth_name` (String) Sets the BIG-IP authentication realm name.
- `auth_pam_dashboard_timeout` (Boolean) Sets whether or not the BIG-IP dashboard will timeout.
- `auth_pam_idle_timeout` (Number) Sets the GUI timeout for automatic logout, in seconds.
- `auth_pam_validate_ip` (Boolean) Sets the authPamValidateIp setting.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fast_cgi_timeout` (Number) Sets the timeout of FastCGI.
- `hostname_lookup` (Boolean) Sets whether or not to display the hostname, if possible.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `log_level` (String) Sets the minimum HTTPD log level.
- `max_clients` (Number) Sets the maximum number of clients that can connect to the GUI at once.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `redirect_http_to_https` (Boolean) Whether or not to redirect HTTP requests to the GUI to HTTPS.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `ssl_cipher_suite` (String) Specifies the ciphers the system uses. The values in the suite are separated by colons (:). Can be specified in either a string or list form. The list form is the recommended way to provide the cipher suite. See examples for usage. Use the value `default` to set the cipher suite to the system default. This value is equivalent to specifying a list of `ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256, ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384,ECDHE-RSA-AES128-SHA,ECDHE-RSA-AES256-SHA, ECDHE-RSA-AES128-SHA256,ECDHE-RSA-AES256-SHA384,ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256, ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384,ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-SHA,ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-SHA, ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-SHA256,ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-SHA384,AES128-GCM-SHA256, AES256-GCM-SHA384,AES128-SHA,AES256-SHA,AES128-SHA256,AES256-SHA256, ECDHE-RSA-DES-CBC3-SHA,ECDHE-ECDSA-DES-CBC3-SHA,DES-CBC3-SHA`.
- `ssl_port` (Number) The HTTPS port on which the system should listen.
- `ssl_protocols` (String) The list of SSL protocols to accept on the management console. A space-separated list of tokens in the format accepted by the Apache mod_ssl SSLProtocol directive. Can be specified in either a string or list form. The list form is the recommended way to provide the cipher suite. See examples for usage. Use the value `default` to set the SSL protocols to the system default. This value is equivalent to specifying a list of `all,-SSLv2,-SSLv3`.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `ssl_cipher_suite_list` (String) List of the new ciphers the system uses.
